docs.title: Karva (0.0.18)
docs.text: A Python test framework, written in Rust.

docs.title: karva
docs.text:
A Python test runner.

Usage 
karva <COMMAND>
Commands karva test

Run tests

karva version

Display Karva's version

karva help

Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

docs.title: karva test
docs.text:
Run tests

Usage 
karva test [OPTIONS] [PATH]...
Arguments PATHS

List of files, directories, or test functions to test [default: the project root]

Options --help, -h

Print help

--test-prefix, -p test-prefix

The prefix of the test functions

[default: test]

--verbose, -v

Use verbose output (or -vv and -vvv for more verbose output)

--watch

Run in watch mode

docs.title: karva version
docs.text:
Display Karva's version

Usage 
karva version
Options --help, -h

Print help

docs.title: karva help
docs.text:
Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Usage 
karva help [COMMAND]

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Karva is available as karva on PyPI.

Use karva directly with uvx:

uvx karva test
uvx karva version

Or install karva with uv, or pip:

# With uv.
uv tool install karva@latest

# Add karva to your project.
uv add --dev karva

# With pip.
pip install karva

Once installed, you can use karva to run your tests:

karva test

Or to get the version of karva you're using:

karva version

docs.title: Tutorial
docs.text: This tutorial will walk you through the basics of using Karva.

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text:
We will first create a new project using uv.

uv init --lib calculator

This will give us a project that looks like this:

calculator
├── pyproject.toml
├── README.md
└── src
    └── calculator
        ├── __init__.py
        └── py.typed

We can then create our core logic in src/calculator/__init__.py.

class Calculator:
    def add(self, a: int, b: int) -> int:
        return a + b

We can then create our tests in tests/test_add.py.

from calculator import Calculator

def test_add():
    calculator = Calculator()
    assert calculator.add(1, 2) == 3

Then, we'll add karva to our project.

uv add --dev karva

We can then run our tests with uv run karva test.

uv run karva test

This will give us a report of our tests.

All checks passed!
